commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: No repair job is needed; the next action is contributor or maintainer-provided real behavior proof for the gateway/WebSocket path before merge.; Cleared: No security or supply-chain changes were introduced; the privacy-sensitive reasoning broadcast remains gated by thinkingLevel !== "off" and silentExpected in the diff and tests. (sha=76b1df49cdb66f5c45f118e1a8c4679300781b7c)
commands.review_summary:
The PR decouples embedded agent thinking-event broadcasts from the optional channel reasoning callback, adds four regression tests, and updates reasoning/Telegram docs for cross-client streaming.

PR surface: Source +1, Tests +110, Docs +5. Total +116 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: current main sets `streamReasoning` false and returns before `emitAgentEvent` whenever `onReasoningStream` is absent. I did not run tests in this read-only review, but the guarded path is direct and high-confidence.
commands.review_followup: No repair job is needed; the next action is contributor or maintainer-provided real behavior proof for the gateway/WebSocket path before merge.

target.title: fix(agents): broadcast agent stream=thinking to gateway clients without channel callback (supersedes #79687)
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

Gateway / WebSocket clients (webchat, Control UI, ACP, plain WS, `chat.send`-originated runs such as cron auto-replies and heartbeats) never receive live `agent.stream === "thinking"` events even with `reasoningMode: "stream"`. `streamReasoning` is gated on `typeof params.onReasoningStream === "function"`, but those code paths do not supply that callback — only Telegram / Mattermost integrations do — so live thinking is silently dropped.

Why does this matter now?

The bug has been observed and re-reported repeatedly. Issues / PRs that track the same root cause: #5086, #42261, #15918, #46121, #47613, #52642, #61469, #79687, #80543, #80956. None has merged. #79687 (the most complete attempt) is open, has `proof: supplied` / `proof: sufficient`, ClawSweeper marked the patch `correct (0.86)`, and its only outstanding item is doc drift in `docs/tools/thinking.md`. This PR finishes that work end-to-end so reviewers have one PR to evaluate.

What is the intended outcome?

- `/reasoning stream` produces live thinking on every gateway client, not only Telegram
- The `thinkingLevel: "off"` opt-out keeps hard-disabling the broadcast
- `silentExpected` runs stay silent
- Telegram / Mattermost integrations keep getting the `onReasoningStream` callback unchanged
- Public docs match the runtime contract

What is intentionally out of scope?

- No change to `/reasoning on` or `/reasoning off` behavior
- No change to existing Telegram preview / draft-bubble logic
- No change to provider-side reasoning extraction or `<think>` parsing
- No change to `agent.stream === "assistant"` deltas

What does success look like?

- Webchat / WS / ACP clients render reasoning incrementally during a turn
- `thinkingLevel: "off"` users see no reasoning anywhere, including the new path
- Telegram users see the same draft-bubble streaming as before
- Existing 39 tests in `subscribeembeddedagentsession.test.ts` still pass; 4 new regression cases pass

What should reviewers focus on?

- `src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.ts:146-150` — `streamReasoning` no longer requires the callback, but `canShowReasoning` is preserved
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.ts:925` — `emitReasoningStream` short-circuits on `!state.streamReasoning` only; callback is now optional via `?.()`
- The 4 new regression tests in `embedded-agent-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-agent-session.subscribeembeddedagentsession.test.ts`

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #42261
Closes #5086

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #79687 (this PR finishes the doc drift item ClawSweeper flagged on #79687)
Related #47613 (earlier attempt; missed `canShowReasoning` and tests)
Related #46121, #15918, #52642, #61469, #80543, #80956 (prior duplicates closed without merge)

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No. This is a community-driven follow-up; #79687 already has bot-flagged `proof: supplied` / `proof: sufficient` and Codex review judged the runtime patch correct (0.86 confidence). This PR addresses the only remaining ClawSweeper blocker (docs) plus consolidates the runtime + tests + docs in one diff so review is single-pass.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Webchat / WS clients receive live `agent.stream === "thinking"` events during `reasoningMode: "stream"` runs.
- Real environment tested: macOS arm64, Node v24.14.0, OpenClaw upstream `main` at `d1577a2ff2`. Patch applied on top, integrated into a downstream Electron client (FinClaw) over the gateway WebSocket.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. Apply this PR on top of `upstream/main`
  2. `pnpm install`
  3. Build and run the gateway: `node openclaw.mjs gateway --port 24881`
  4. Use any non-Telegram client (webchat / WS / a local Electron app) that consumes `agent.stream` events; in the active session run `/reasoning stream` then send a prompt that triggers a reasoning-capable model (e.g. GPT-5 / Claude with a thinking level).
  5. Observe live `agent.stream === "thinking"` events with cumulative `data.text` and incremental `data.delta` arriving during the model's thinking phase, before the final assistant text.
- Evidence after fix:
  - 43/43 tests pass in `embedded-agent-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-agent-session.subscribeembeddedagentsession.test.ts`, including the 4 new regression cases.
  - Downstream client (FinClaw) renders thinking inline, replacing the previous "agent appears frozen" UX described in #42261.
- Observed result after fix: gateway subscribers receive `{ stream: "thinking", data: { text, delta } }` for every reasoning chunk; final answer still arrives as before; `thinkingLevel: "off"` and `silentExpected` produce zero thinking events.
- What was not tested:
  - I did not exercise live Telegram or Mattermost end-to-end (no test channel access). The Telegram path is covered by an in-process regression test that asserts `onReasoningStream` is still invoked when supplied; behavior parity is otherwise inferred from the diff's locality (the only Telegram-touching change is making the callback optional).
  - Cross-provider matrix beyond OpenAI Codex / GPT-5 and Anthropic Claude was not exercised; the change does not depend on provider behavior.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints:
  - End-to-end Telegram / Mattermost verification requires bot credentials I do not have. The bot path is covered by unit-level regression test #4.
- Before evidence (optional): #42261, #5086, and #79687 all describe the same observable symptom (clients see no thinking events, agent looks frozen).

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run \
  --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts \
  src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-agent-session.subscribeembeddedagentsession.test.ts
# 43/43 passed (39 pre-existing + 4 new regression cases)
```

What regression coverage was added or updated?

Added a `describe("agent stream=thinking emission without onReasoningStream callback", ...)` block with 4 cases:

1. `broadcasts agent stream=thinking to gateway clients with no callback supplied` — chat.send / WS path (the original bug).
2. `does not leak reasoning when thinkingLevel is 'off' even without a callback` — verifies `canShowReasoning` still hard-gates the broadcast.
3. `respects silentExpected and emits no thinking events even without a callback` — verifies the `silentExpected` short-circuit.
4. `still invokes onReasoningStream when supplied (Telegram / Mattermost path)` — verifies the existing channel callback path is unbroken; both `emitAgentEvent` and `onReasoningStream` fire exactly once.

What failed before this fix, if known?

Before the patch, `streamReasoning` was `false` whenever `onReasoningStream` was undefined, so `emitReasoningStream` short-circuited and never reached `emitAgentEvent`. Test #1 reproduces that: prior to the runtime change, `emitAgentEventSpy.mock.calls.filter(stream === "thinking")` returns `[]`. With the fix, it returns 2 entries with the correct `data.text` / `data.delta`.

If no test was added, why not?

N/A — 4 regression tests added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes — but in a strictly additive way for clients that opted in via `/reasoning stream`. Clients that did not opt in (`reasoningMode !== "stream"`) see no change. Clients with `thinkingLevel: "off"` see no change (still nothing). Telegram / Mattermost behavior is unchanged.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No. No config schema change, no migration. The `/reasoning stream` directive parser, storage, and resolution order are untouched.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No. The patch only changes which subscribers receive an existing internal event. The `thinkingLevel: "off"` privacy gate is explicitly preserved (regression-tested) so reasoning cannot leak to a session that opted out.

What is the highest-risk area?

Accidental reasoning leak when a user has `thinkingLevel: "off"` — this is the failure mode #47613 was rejected for. Mitigated below.

How is that risk mitigated?

- `streamReasoning: reasoningMode === "stream" && canShowReasoning` keeps the existing `thinkingLevel: "off"` gate.
- Test case #2 asserts zero thinking events when `thinkingLevel: "off"` even without a callback.
- The diff is 13 lines in `embedded-agent-subscribe.ts`; no other gating logic moved.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Maintainer review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- Maintainer / human approver decision (Copilot review on the predecessor #79687 had no findings; ClawSweeper judged the predecessor patch `correct (0.86)`).
- Live Telegram / Mattermost regression evidence is not in this PR; the Telegram callback path is covered by unit test #4. If a maintainer can run a live Telegram check, that would be the only remaining verification gap.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- ClawSweeper on #79687 (P3 docs blocker): `docs/tools/thinking.md` no longer says `/reasoning stream` is Telegram-only; the new wording describes cross-client behavior, the `thinkingLevel: "off"` hard-gate, and links from `docs/channels/telegram.md`.
- ClawSweeper on #47613 (low-confidence safety concern about `canShowReasoning` after rebase): the PR keeps `canShowReasoning` in the `streamReasoning` expression and adds an explicit regression test (case #2).
- ClawSweeper on #47613 (missing no-callback regression test): added (cases #1, #2, #3).
- Codex review on #42261 (build the broadcast on `reasoningMode === "stream"` + visibility gate, emit before optional callback): implemented exactly that — `emitAgentEvent` runs first, then `onReasoningStream?.()`.
target.author: likedege
